findings.title: CWE-502: Unsafe deserialization at line 38
findings.description: Unsafe pickle deserialization at L38: `return pickle.loads(zlib.decompress(f.read()))`. If the data comes from an untrusted source, an attacker can achieve RCE.
findings.suggestion: Use JSON. If pickle is required, verify data integrity with HMAC before loading.
findings.auto_fix:
BEFORE: return pickle.loads(zlib.decompress(f.read()))
AFTER:                      return json.loads(zlib.decompress(f.read()))

rule.title: Python unsafe deserialization pattern
rule.summary: Flags direct use of unsafe deserializers such as `pickle.loads`, `marshal.loads`, or unsafe `yaml.load`.
rule.remediation: Use JSON or verify integrity and safety before deserializing.

findings.title: CWE-502: Unsafe deserialization at line 71

AFTER:                          previous_value = json.loads(zlib.decompress(f.read()))

rule.title: Python unsafe deserialization pattern

rule.remediation: Use JSON or verify integrity and safety before deserializing.

findings.title: Implicit None return in _cull() — can fall off end
findings.description: `_cull()` has 1 branches that return a value and 1 that return None implicitly. Callers that do not check for None will encounter AttributeError or incorrect logic.
findings.suggestion: Ensure all code paths return an explicit value, or annotate the return type.

rule.title: Inconsistent return paths causing implicit None
rule.summary: Flags functions whose branches mix explicit return values with implicit `None` fall-through.
rule.remediation: Ensure all paths return an explicit value or annotate the optional return contract clearly.

findings.title: CWE-22: Path traversal in _key_to_file() via os.path.join()
findings.description: `_key_to_file()` passes `join` (dynamic/path-like input) to `os.path.join()` at L130 without sanitization. `../` sequences can escape the intended directory. (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Sanitize: `from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename; safe = secure_filename(filename)`. Verify resolved path: `assert os.path.realpath(p).startswith(BASE_DIR)`.

rule.title: Python path join with unsanitized user input
rule.summary: Flags `os.path.join` or similar path composition with tainted user input.
rule.remediation: Normalize against a safe root and reject any resolved path that escapes it.

findings.title: CWE-22: Path traversal in touch() via open()
findings.description: `touch()` passes `_key_to_file` (user-controlled) to file open at L65 without path sanitization. `../` sequences can escape the intended directory and read/write arbitrary files. (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Sanitize: `from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename; safe = secure_filename(filename)`. Verify resolved path: `assert os.path.realpath(p).startswith(BASE_DIR)`.
findings.auto_fix:
BEFORE: with open(self._key_to_file(key, version), "r+b") as f:
AFTER:              safe_path = Path(BASE_DIR, self).resolve()
                    assert str(safe_path).startswith(str(BASE_DIR))
                    with open(safe_path) as f:

rule.title: Python file open with tainted path
rule.summary: Flags `open()` or `Path.*` file operations that use user-controlled paths without confinement.
rule.remediation: Secure filenames and verify the resolved path remains under the expected base directory.

findings.title: CWE-338: Weak PRNG (Math.random) in security context at line 17
findings.description: `Math.random()` used near security-sensitive code at L17: `*/var B={astral:!1,namespacing:!0},w={},j={},k={},S=[],O="default",R="class",_={default:/\`. Math.random is NOT cryptographically secure. (minified bundle without source map; confidence downgraded to low)
findings.suggestion: Use `crypto.randomBytes(32)` (Node.js) or `crypto.getRandomValues()` (browser).

rule.title: Weak PRNG (Math.random) in security context
rule.summary: `Math.random()` used near security-sensitive code.
rule.remediation: Use `crypto.randomBytes(32)` (Node.js) or `crypto.getRandomValues()` (browser).

findings.title: CWE-1321: Prototype pollution risk at line 17
findings.description: Object modification using `__proto__`, `constructor.prototype`, or `Object.assign` with unchecked user input at L17: `*/var B={astral:!1,namespacing:!0},w={},j={},k={},S=[],O="default",R="class",_={default:/\`. (minified bundle without source map; confidence downgraded to low) (vendor/minified asset heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Validate keys, strip `__proto__` / `constructor`, and prefer safe merge patterns.

rule.title: Prototype pollution risk
rule.summary: Object modification using `__proto__`, `constructor.prototype`, or `Object.assign` with unchecked user input.
rule.remediation: Validate keys, strip `__proto__` / `constructor`, and prefer safe merge patterns.

findings.title: CWE-1333: Potential ReDoS — catastrophic backtracking regex at line 17
findings.description: Regex with nested quantifiers or ambiguous alternation at L17: `*/var B={astral:!1,namespacing:!0},w={},j={},k={},S=[],O="default",R="class",_={default:/\`. (minified bundle without source map; confidence downgraded to low) (vendor/minified asset heuristic downgraded) (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Use safe regex patterns or a non-backtracking engine.

rule.title: Potential ReDoS — catastrophic backtracking regex
rule.summary: Regex with nested quantifiers or ambiguous alternation.
rule.remediation: Use safe regex patterns or a non-backtracking engine.

findings.description: Untrusted data flows from `data` (function parameter (likely untrusted), L25) to `pickle.loads()` at L29 without sanitization. An attacker can exploit this to execute arbitrary code via crafted serialized objects.
findings.suggestion: Use json.loads() for untrusted data. If pickle is required, verify HMAC first.
findings.auto_fix:

AFTER:              return json.loads(data)

rule.title: Python unsafe deserialization via tainted input
rule.summary: Tracks untrusted data into unsafe deserializers such as pickle or yaml.load-style sinks.
rule.remediation: Prefer JSON or verify integrity before deserializing attacker-controlled content.

findings.title: CWE-307: No rate limiting on authentication route at line 34
findings.description: Authentication route `/login` at L34 (`app.post("/login", sessionHandler.handleLoginRequest)`) has no rate-limiting middleware in scope. An attacker can brute-force credentials, OTPs, or tokens.
findings.suggestion: Apply rate limiting, for example `const limiter = rateLimit({ windowMs: 15*60*1000, max: 10 })`, or attach a route-scoped/per-prefix limiter before the auth handler.

rule.title: JS auth route missing rate limiting
rule.summary: Flags login-like routes with no obvious rate-limiter middleware in scope.
rule.remediation: Apply a per-IP or per-account rate limiter before the auth handler.

findings.title: CWE-352: State-changing route may lack CSRF protection at line 34
findings.description: POST/PUT/DELETE route defined at L34 without detectable CSRF token validation. Cross-site request forgery can force authenticated users to perform unintended actions.
findings.suggestion: Use csurf middleware or implement CSRF token validation for all mutating routes.

rule.title: CWE-352: State-changing route may lack CSRF protection at line 34
rule.summary: Flags browser-exposed state mutations that appear to lack CSRF defenses.
rule.remediation: Require CSRF tokens or same-site protections for cookie-authenticated browser flows.

rule.summary: Flags login-like routes with no obvious rate-limiter middleware in scope.

findings.title: CWE-798: Hardcoded credential at line 6
findings.description: A credential, key, or password appears to be hardcoded at L6: `zapApiKey: "v9dn0balpqas1pcc281tn5ood1",`. Commit history will permanently expose this credential even after removal.
findings.suggestion: Move secrets to environment variables: `process.env.API_KEY`. Use a secrets manager for production.

rule.remediation: Move secrets to environment variables: `process.env.API_KEY`. Use a secrets manager for production.

findings.title: CWE-601: Open redirect in response_add() via redirect()
findings.description: `response_add()` passes user-controlled `post_url_continue` to `redirect()` at L1494. An attacker can craft a URL that redirects victims to a malicious site for phishing or credential theft.
findings.suggestion: Validate redirect targets against an allowlist of safe paths/domains. Use `url_for()` for internal redirects. Check with `url_has_allowed_host_and_scheme(url, allowed_hosts)`.

rule.title: Python open redirect via redirect()
rule.summary: Flags `redirect()` calls that forward user-controlled URLs without allowlist validation.
rule.remediation: Use `url_for()` for internal redirects or allowlist external destinations explicitly.

findings.title: CWE-95: Code Injection via exec() in handle()
findings.description: Untrusted data flows from `sys.stdin.read()` (stdin, L271) to `exec()` at L271 without sanitization. An attacker can exploit this to execute arbitrary Python code in the application context.
findings.suggestion: Avoid eval/exec. Use ast.literal_eval() for data, or a safe expression evaluator.

rule.remediation: Replace eval-like execution with safe parsing or explicit dispatch.

rule.remediation: Catch narrower exception types or log and re-raise unexpected errors.

findings.description: `traceback.print_exc()` or equivalent at L90 may leak internal paths, versions, and logic to end users.
findings.suggestion: Use `logger.exception()` server-side and return a generic error message to clients.

rule.summary: Flags `traceback.print_exc()`, `sys.exc_info()` in response bodies, or debug error handlers leaking internals.
rule.remediation: Use generic error handlers in production; log details with `logger.exception()` server-side only.

findings.suggestion: Catch specific exception types, or log and re-raise broad catches.

rule.title: Broad exception catch without re-raise
rule.summary: Flags broad exception handlers that suppress unexpected failures instead of surfacing them.
rule.remediation: Catch narrower exception types or log and re-raise unexpected errors.

findings.title: CWE-470: Externally-controlled method dispatch in dispatch() at line 140
findings.description: `getattr()` is called with a tainted attribute name from `method` (function parameter (likely untrusted), L134). An attacker can invoke arbitrary methods on the target object, potentially accessing `__class__`, `__subclasses__`, or other introspection gadgets. (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Validate attribute names against an explicit allowlist before calling getattr(): `ALLOWED = {'view', 'edit'}; assert attr in ALLOWED; getattr(obj, attr)()`

rule.title: Externally-controlled method/module dispatch
rule.summary: Detects getattr(), __import__(), and importlib.import_module() called with tainted attribute or module names.
rule.remediation: Validate attribute/module names against an explicit allowlist before dynamic dispatch.

findings.title: CWE-862: Django CBV `TemplateView` exposes get with no auth mixin
findings.description: `TemplateView` implements `get` but does not inherit an auth-enforcing mixin and has no `@method_decorator(login_required)` / permission decorator. Class-based view methods can be reached without authentication. (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Add `LoginRequiredMixin` / `PermissionRequiredMixin`, or protect dispatch/methods with `@method_decorator(login_required, name='dispatch')`.

rule.title: Django class-based view missing auth mixin
rule.summary: Flags Django class-based views implementing get/post without LoginRequiredMixin, PermissionRequiredMixin, or an equivalent auth decorator.
rule.remediation: Add LoginRequiredMixin / PermissionRequiredMixin or protect dispatch with method_decorator(login_required).
known_limitations: Heuristics are strongest on common Flask/FastAPI and Express/Router-style patterns.

findings.title: CWE-862: Django CBV `RedirectView` exposes get, post with no auth mixin
findings.description: `RedirectView` implements `get, post` but does not inherit an auth-enforcing mixin and has no `@method_decorator(login_required)` / permission decorator. Class-based view methods can be reached without authentication. (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Add `LoginRequiredMixin` / `PermissionRequiredMixin`, or protect dispatch/methods with `@method_decorator(login_required, name='dispatch')`.

trace.label: CBV methods `get, post` exposed without auth

known_limitations: Heuristics are strongest on common Flask/FastAPI and Express/Router-style patterns.

rule.title: Broad exception catch without re-raise

findings.title: CWE-200: Traceback/error details exposed to users at line 1010
findings.description: `traceback.print_exc()` or equivalent at L1010 may leak internal paths, versions, and logic to end users.
findings.suggestion: Use `logger.exception()` server-side and return a generic error message to clients.

rule.title: Stack trace or internal error details exposed to users
rule.summary: Flags `traceback.print_exc()`, `sys.exc_info()` in response bodies, or debug error handlers leaking internals.
rule.remediation: Use generic error handlers in production; log details with `logger.exception()` server-side only.

findings.title: CWE-22: Path traversal in auto_find_instance_path() via os.path.join()
findings.description: `auto_find_instance_path()` passes `package_path` (dynamic/path-like input) to `os.path.join()` at L517 without sanitization. `../` sequences can escape the intended directory. (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Sanitize: `from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename; safe = secure_filename(filename)`. Verify resolved path: `assert os.path.realpath(p).startswith(BASE_DIR)`.

rule.title: Python path join with unsanitized user input
rule.summary: Flags `os.path.join` or similar path composition with tainted user input.
rule.remediation: Normalize against a safe root and reject any resolved path that escapes it.

findings.title: CWE-95: Code injection via eval() at line 32
findings.description: `eval()` is called with dynamic content at L32: `eval(req.body.preTax)`. If the argument is attacker-influenced, arbitrary JavaScript can execute.
findings.suggestion: Remove `eval()`. Parse data with `JSON.parse()` or use a safe expression interpreter.

rule.title: Code injection via eval()
rule.summary: `eval()` called.
rule.remediation: Eliminate eval(). Use JSON.parse() for data, or a safe expression library.

findings.title: CWE-798: Hardcoded Auth token at line 246
findings.description: A Auth token is hardcoded in source code at L246. This is visible in version control. Rotate this credential immediately. (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, .env excluded from git).

rule.title: Python hardcoded credential
rule.summary: Flags likely credentials, API keys, or signing secrets committed directly to source.
rule.remediation: Move secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager and rotate any exposed values.

findings.title: Excessive complexity in __init__() (CC=24)
findings.description: `__init__()` at L20 has cyclomatic complexity 24. Functions above 15 are hard to test, debug, and maintain.
findings.suggestion: Extract sub-functions for distinct logic branches or use lookup tables.

rule.title: Cyclomatic complexity hotspot
rule.summary: Flags overly branchy functions that are statistically harder to review, test, and secure.
rule.remediation: Split complex control flow into smaller helpers with single responsibilities.
